reasoning: The most recent value of the CBOE Crude Oil ETF Volatility Index (OVXCLS) as of Dec 5 is 32.06. This reflects a recent steep drop following the OPEC+ meeting (which resolved uncertainty by largely maintaining status quo with a modest output increase). Historically, 32.06 is on the lower end of the index's range over the past year (which typically fluctuates between 30 and 49). Because volatility indices are inherently mean-reverting and bounded on the downside, the probability of exceeding this relatively low threshold is greater than 0.5. The FRED statistical trend analysis estimates a 66% chance of the index being higher than 32.06 by Dec 14, largely driven by expected mean reversion and seasonal behavior. Therefore, I anchor the 1-week forecast at 0.65. Following the structural guideline that uncertainty must increase (probabilities converge to 0.5) as the forecast horizon extends, the estimates for Jan 6 and Mar 7 are reduced to 0.58 and 0.52, respectively.
